extraction.one_line_summary: CHAVES Esteban attacked the INEOS train with 1km remaining on Porte climb, winning by 7 seconds as INEOS prioritized protecting their 1-2-3 GC over the stage win.
tactical_patterns: KEMNA Leonard infiltrated early break only 1:30 down on GC, forcing INEOS to chase all day despite wanting break to succeed.
INEOS sacrificed stage win to protect three GC podium positions, riding tempo during KARAPAZ chain drop rather than attacking.
INEOS deployed ROHAN Dennis and CASTRO Viejo to neutralize valley before final climb, preventing break from establishing decisive gap on descent.

rider_signals.CHAVES Esteban: positive: First win since 2019 Giro, climbing level suggests BikeExchange has genuine Grand Tour GC asset if form holds
rider_signals.KEMNA Leonard: positive: Tactical nuisance value proven by forcing INEOS into hard pace all day from mid-race break infiltration despite GC proximity
rider_signals.YATES Simon: negative: Failed to show Tirreno-Adriatico form specifically on Prati di Tivo comparison, non-factor in mountain stages
rider_signals.FROOME Chris: negative: Altitude exposure at Valthorens clearly affected performance, dropped halfway up penultimate climb
rider_signals.DENNIS Rohan: positive: World TT champion climbing well enough to lead mountain chase, key weapon in INEOS valley neutralization tactic
